Title: Comparative genomics of geographically distant Fusarium fujikuroi isolates revealed two distinct pathotypes correlating with secondary metabolite profiles

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: FUM1 is a key diagnostic and intervention target for distinguishing and suppressing the stunting/withering pathotype of Fusarium fujikuroi on rice. Disrupting FUM1, ideally together with FUB1, reduces virulence and delays symptom development. In pathogen surveillance, FUM1 expression/high fumonisin production can help identify high-risk stunting isolates. Because symptom outcome also depends on the absence of fungal GA production, FUM1 should be interpreted jointly with GA-pathway status.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the polyketide synthase step that builds the fumonisin backbone for fumonisin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fumonisin accumulation (FB1/FB2) and contributes to rice seedling stunting/withering in strain B14.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion in B14 and B20; add-back complementation in B14; exogenous FB1 feeding; qPCR/Northern blot/RNA-seq expression analysis; HPLC/LC-MS metabolite profiling in vitro and in planta; rice seedling pathogenicity assays.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the toxin scaffold required for target product biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low nitrogen (6 mM glutamine), high nitrogen (60 mM glutamine), rice infection in planta (7 dpi), exogenous FB1 treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In infected rice seedlings/roots at 7 dpi, expression is high in B14 and other stunting-type isolates; low or nearly silent in bakanae-type isolates such as B20 under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High under low nitrogen and in planta in B14/stunting-type F. fujikuroi isolates; weak in most bakanae-type isolates.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing/annotation, RNA-seq (GEO: GSE89480), metabolite profiling, comparative genomics (ENA study: PRJEB14872)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The entire fumonisin cluster is present in most F. fujikuroi isolates, but B20 and C1995 lack several cluster genes. FUM1 expression correlates strongly with the stunting pathotype. Deletion reduced but did not fully abolish disease, indicating additional factors such as fusaric acid and GA deficiency contribute.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Fumonisin mycotoxin branch of fungal secondary metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: For the fungus, FUM1 loss reduces virulence/stunting capacity. No host breeding tradeoff assessed directly.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Fumonisin precursor/backbone leading to FB1 and FB2
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported by deletion phenotypes; pathway-initiation/committed enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUM1 is highly expressed in the stunting isolate B14 and in other stunting-type field isolates, but weakly expressed in bakanae isolates. 2. B14 produces high amounts of fumonisins in vitro and in rice, and Δfum1 mutants show delayed/reduced stunting and withering. 3. Exogenous FB1 partially restores the wild-type stunting phenotype, supporting a causal role for fumonisins in symptom development.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture filtrate; infected rice seedlings/roots and shoots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to fungal virulence and host growth inhibition/stunting/withering.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: FUB1 is a useful pathogen-target gene for reducing stunting/withering symptoms caused by B14-like Fusarium fujikuroi isolates. Stacking FUB1 disruption with FUM1 disruption provides stronger attenuation than either single mutant. For diagnostics and control, FUB1 should be considered as a co-determinant rather than a sole cause.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the polyketide synthase step required for fusaric acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fusaric acid production and contributes to stunting/withering symptoms in B14, especially together with fumonisin production and absence of GAs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion in B14 and B20; add-back complementation in B14; exogenous fusaric acid feeding; RNA-seq expression analysis; metabolite profiling in vitro and in planta; rice seedling pathogenicity assays.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the toxin scaffold required for target product biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low nitrogen, high nitrogen, rice infection in planta, exogenous fusaric acid treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In infected rice, fusaric acid pathway genes show similar expression across isolates; B14 is not unique for expression, but deletion demonstrates contribution to symptoms in the B14 background.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed under tested in vitro and in planta conditions in multiple isolates; product commonly detected, especially under high nitrogen in vitro and in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing/annotation, RNA-seq (GEO: GSE89480), metabolite profiling, comparative genomics (ENA study: PRJEB14872)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although fusaric acid had long been speculated to cause stunting, this study provided experimental support by showing that Δfub1 mutants have delayed symptom development and exogenous fusaric acid restores stronger symptoms. However, fusaric acid alone is insufficient to explain all phenotype differences because many isolates produce it.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of FUB1 attenuates fungal virulence in the B14 background. No direct host-side tradeoffs reported.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported by gene deletion and complementation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUB1 is required for fusaric acid biosynthesis and contributes to the stunting/withering phenotype of B14. 2. Δfub1 mutants of B14 show delayed disease development, and add-back restores the wild-type phenotype. 3. The stronger effect of the Δfum1/Δfub1 double mutant indicates fusaric acid acts together with fumonisins and GA deficiency in pathotype determination.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture filtrate; infected rice seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to host toxicity, virulence, and symptom severity including stunting/withering.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CPS/KS is a robust molecular marker for bakanae-type F. fujikuroi isolates that cause elongation through fungal GA production. In disease diagnostics, low CPS/KS expression combined with high FUM1 expression identifies stunting-type isolates. Pathogen-side disruption of CPS/KS abolishes bakanae symptoms, but host engineering application is not the focus.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the bifunctional cyclization of geranylgeranyl diphosphate through copalyl diphosphate to ent-kaurene/early diterpene GA precursor steps in the fungal GA pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fungal gibberellin production and causes bakanae-type elongation of infected rice seedlings; lack of expression/production in B14 is associated with absence of bakanae symptoms.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq, Northern blot analysis, HPLC-DAD/LC-MS metabolite profiling, qPCR of field isolates, pathogenicity assays, and reference to B20 Δcps/ks mutant phenotype in culture-filtrate experiments.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the core diterpene precursor required for GA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low nitrogen strongly favors expression/GA production in most isolates; rice infection in planta; exogenous GA3 treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In infected rice roots at 7 dpi, GA biosynthetic genes are among the highest expressed genes in bakanae isolates but are hardly expressed in B14 and stunting-type isolates.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High under low nitrogen in eight of nine F. fujikuroi isolates; absent or nearly absent in B14.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing/annotation, RNA-seq (GEO: GSE89480), qPCR of field isolates, metabolite profiling, comparative genomics (ENA study: PRJEB14872)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: B14 retains the complete GA cluster but shows almost no expression and no detectable GAs. Exogenous GA3 could convert the Δfum1/Δfub1 double mutant toward bakanae-like elongation, showing that GA action is masked by fumonisin/fusaric-acid-driven toxicity in wild-type B14.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: For the pathogen, GA production is linked to bakanae-type elongation rather than stunting; lack of GA in B14 shifts pathotype. No direct host tradeoff measured.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Early diterpene GA precursor (ent-kaurene pathway intermediate)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/diagnostic step for fungal GA production in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B14; B20; multiple global and Korean field isolates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPS/KS expression and fungal GA production correlate with the bakanae pathotype across global and Korean field isolates. 2. B14 contains the GA cluster but shows almost no CPS/KS expression and no detectable GAs in vitro or in rice. 3. GA deficiency is a major component of the stunting pathotype, and exogenous GA3 can induce elongation only when fumonisin and fusaric acid biosynthesis are simultaneously removed.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture filtrate; infected rice seedlings/roots and shoots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stimulates plant stem elongation and causes bakanae symptoms.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PKS51 is a lineage-specific marker for the stunting subclade of F. fujikuroi and can be used in PCR-based surveillance to distinguish pathotypes. Because PKS51 deletion increases disease severity, its product may act as an avirulence/attenuation factor; therefore, its presence should not be interpreted as a simple virulence determinant. Further metabolite identification is required before deployment in engineering or diagnostics beyond pathotype typing.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Presumed to synthesize the backbone of an unknown polyketide product encoded by a B14-specific gene cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts negatively on symptom severity: deletion of PKS51 causes earlier and more severe stunting/withering in B14, suggesting the unknown product attenuates disease or behaves like an avirulence factor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics showing B14-specific presence; RNA-seq showing strong in planta expression; targeted gene deletion in B14; rice pathogenicity assays; PCR marker analysis across field isolates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for biosynthesis of an unknown secondary metabolite that modulates disease outcome.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Strongly expressed during rice infection (in planta); little/no relevant product characterization under in vitro conditions reported.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Exclusively and strongly expressed in planta in B14 during rice infection.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present only in B14-like stunting isolates; absent from bakanae-type isolates examined.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing/annotation, RNA-seq (GEO: GSE89480), comparative genomics, PCR-based field-isolate typing (ENA study: PRJEB14872)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PKS51 is unique to B14 among analyzed isolates and absent from other examined FFC members. Its cluster-specific TF (FFB14_06367) is highly expressed in planta. The product is unknown, but the phenotype of ΔPKS51 suggests attenuation rather than promotion of virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of PKS51 causes more severe and earlier symptoms, implying that the pathway product may reduce aggressiveness or trigger host recognition.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme of a B14-specific cluster; decisive for production of the unknown pathway metabolite.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PKS51 is a unique B14-specific PKS gene present in stunting-type but not bakanae-type isolates. 2. The gene cluster is specifically expressed during rice infection, implicating it in host interaction. 3. Deletion of PKS51 causes earlier and stronger stunting/withering, suggesting its unknown product attenuates disease or may function analogously to an avirulence determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Likely infected rice tissue during infection; not chemically identified
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Putatively modulates host-pathogen interaction and attenuates disease severity.
